Add a QR code feature to the PixelStudio project so that each invoice includes a QR code linking to the client’s photo gallery.

Project context:

* PixelStudio is a photography management system.
* Frontend: React + Vite.
* Backend: Node.js + Express + Prisma + MySQL.
* Each client has a private gallery link generated by the backend.
* Example gallery link: https://yourdomain.com/gallery/:token
* Clients use that link to view and download their photos.

Goal:
When an invoice is generated for a client, include a QR code on the invoice that points to the client’s gallery link. When the client scans the QR code with their phone, it should open their gallery page.

Backend tasks:

1. Ensure each client or gallery has a unique gallery token stored in the database.
2. When generating invoice data, include the gallery URL in the response.
3. The gallery URL format should be:
   https://yourdomain.com/gallery/{token}
4. Make sure the invoice API returns the gallery URL along with other invoice data.

Frontend tasks:

1. Install a QR code library suitable for React (for example: qrcode.react or react-qr-code).
2. Update the invoice preview component so that it:

   * Displays client information
   * Displays invoice details
   * Generates a QR code based on the gallery URL.
3. Place the QR code on the invoice layout in a clear section labeled:
   “Scan to View Your Photos”.

Invoice layout requirements:

* Show:

  * Studio name
  * Invoice number
  * Client name
  * Service type
  * Photo format (Softcopy / Hardcopy / Both)
  * Amount
  * Payment status
  * Date
* Include a QR code box containing the generated QR code.
* Add helper text under the QR code:
  “Scan this QR code to access and download your photos.”

Print requirements:

* The QR code should remain visible and scannable when the invoice is printed.
* Ensure the QR code has enough size (minimum ~150px) for easy scanning.

Example behavior:

* Staff generates invoice.
* Invoice page shows the QR code.
* Client receives the invoice (PDF or printed).
* Client scans the QR code.
* Client is redirected to:
  https://yourdomain.com/gallery/{token}

Important:

* Do not rebuild the entire project.
* Only extend the existing invoice module and UI.
* Keep the code modular and easy to maintain.
* Make the QR code reusable as a component for future use.

Also explain:

* where the QR code component should be placed in the React project
* how the gallery URL should be passed from the backend to the invoice component
* how the QR code can also be included when exporting the invoice as a PDF.
